edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:a08a52b27c10
edges.original_sentence: Higher sucker densities, taller suckers and lower sucker mortality at the 40 cm treatment suggest that increased salvage depth (good mineral soil-root contact) is a prerequisite for successful suckering.

source_spans.text: Higher sucker densities, taller suckers and lower sucker mortality at the 40 cm treatment suggest that increased salvage depth (good mineral soil-root contact) is a prerequisite for successful suckering.

source_document.title: Transfer of live aspen roots as a reclamation technique – Effects of soil depth, root diameter and fine root growth on root suckering ability –

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:b234677cd143
edges.original_sentence: Thus, the improved suckering in the deep treatment strongly supports our hypotheses that soil-root contact plays a crucial role in the success of root suckering from root fragments.

source_spans.text: Thus, the improved suckering in the deep treatment strongly supports our hypotheses that soil-root contact plays a crucial role in the success of root suckering from root fragments.

source_document.title: Transfer of live aspen roots as a reclamation technique – Effects of soil depth, root diameter and fine root growth on root suckering ability –

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:9c80faee4283
edges.original_sentence: Since only a limited amount of carbohydrates can be stored in root fragments, the quick emergence of suckers and the establishment of leaf area are crucial to root and sucker survival. We found that root fragments with emerged suckers attached had considerably higher carbohydrate contents than roots without emerged suckers.

source_spans.text: Since only a limited amount of carbohydrates can be stored in root fragments, the quick emergence of suckers and the establishment of leaf area are crucial to root and sucker survival. We found that root fragments with emerged suckers attached had considerably higher carbohydrate contents than roots without emerged suckers.

source_document.title: Transfer of live aspen roots as a reclamation technique – Effects of soil depth, root diameter and fine root growth on root suckering ability –

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:98989bd944e2
edges.original_sentence: Suckers from root fragments buried at a depth of 40 cm were unsuccessful in reaching the soil surface, indicating that the reserves in the root fragments were not sufficient to allow suckers to grow from a depth greater than 20 cm.

source_spans.text: Suckers from root fragments buried at a depth of 40 cm were unsuccessful in reaching the soil surface, indicating that the reserves in the root fragments were not sufficient to allow suckers to grow from a depth greater than 20 cm.

source_document.title: Transfer of live aspen roots as a reclamation technique – Effects of soil depth, root diameter and fine root growth on root suckering ability –

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:893a7517b889
edges.original_sentence: The presence of fine roots on root fragments appears not to play a positive role in root suckering; indeed, attached fine roots appear to be potentially a liability, as overall sucker numbers (emerged and non-emerged suckers) were three times higher in root fragments that had all their fine roots removed compared to root fragments with fine roots attached.

source_spans.text: The presence of fine roots on root fragments appears not to play a positive role in root suckering; indeed, attached fine roots appear to be potentially a liability, as overall sucker numbers (emerged and non-emerged suckers) were three times higher in root fragments that had all their fine roots removed compared to root fragments with fine roots attached.

source_document.title: Transfer of live aspen roots as a reclamation technique – Effects of soil depth, root diameter and fine root growth on root suckering ability –

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:510269e47eb8
edges.original_sentence: We found that growth of new fine roots was only associated with the presence of emerged suckers.

source_spans.text: We found that growth of new fine roots was only associated with the presence of emerged suckers.

source_document.title: Transfer of live aspen roots as a reclamation technique – Effects of soil depth, root diameter and fine root growth on root suckering ability –

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:7faca38c6238
edges.original_sentence: In our study invasive agricultural species like Alsike clover flourished on the site and overtopped the smaller suckers, which were prone to being smothered under the heavy leaf cover.

source_spans.text: In our study invasive agricultural species like Alsike clover flourished on the site and overtopped the smaller suckers, which were prone to being smothered under the heavy leaf cover.

source_document.title: Transfer of live aspen roots as a reclamation technique – Effects of soil depth, root diameter and fine root growth on root suckering ability –
